free_text: Inelastic scattering of 31-MeV alpha particles from
Zn64 and Zn66
#doi:10.1103/PhysRevC.2.974
The scattered alpha particles were detected by single
semiconductor counters.
Self-supporting metal foils, approximately 1 mg/cm2
thick.
The optical-model parameters were calculated, the
DWBA calculations were done.
OG
The error in the elastic cross section for one
angle relative to another (the relative error) is
estimated at about 3%.
The error in the elastic cross section for one
angle relative to another (the relative error) is
estimated at about 3%.
Presented in the figures. The largest
contributors to the error in the inelastic scattering
are the uncertainties in background subtraction and
sometimes in the peak unfolding procedure. Data
without errors if the relative errors are less than 8%.
